---
title: "Pygame Quest Track"
subtitle: "Master Game Development Through 7 Epic Adventures"
track-id: "pygame"
track-icon: "🎮"
track-color: "#ec4899"
difficulty: "Intermediate"
total-quests: 7
estimated-hours: 30
format:
  live-html:
    toc: false
---

::: {.hero}
# 🎮 Welcome to the Pygame Quest Track!

*Transform your Python skills into playable games! Build 7 complete games from scratch.*
:::

## 🎯 About This Track

Ready to see your code come to life? This track takes you from game development novice to confident game creator. You'll build 7 complete, playable games using Pygame, each introducing new concepts and techniques.

### What You'll Build

- ✅ **Maze Explorer** - Game loops, collision detection, input handling
- ✅ **Bouncing Ball** - Physics simulation, velocity, gravity
- ✅ **Pong Game** - Two-player mechanics, AI opponents
- ✅ **Snake Game** - Grid-based movement, data structures
- ✅ **Flappy Bird** - Infinite scrolling, jump mechanics
- ✅ **Frogger** - Multi-lane systems, platform riding
- ✅ **Dungeon Crawler** - Tile maps, camera systems, AI pathfinding

### Prerequisites

::: {.callout-note}
## Before You Begin

**Python knowledge required** - You should be comfortable with:
- Variables, loops, and functions
- Basic data structures (lists, dictionaries)
- Object-oriented programming basics

**Recommended preparation:**
- [Python Quest Track](../python/index.qmd) (highly recommended)
- Or equivalent Python experience

**New to Python?** [Start with Python Track](../python/index.qmd) first!
:::

---

## 🗺️ Your Game Development Journey

This track contains **7 progressive game projects**, each building on previous skills.

<div class="quest-grid">

::: {.quest-card}
### 🌟 Beginner Games (Quests 1-2)
Learn the fundamentals

**Quest 1: Maze Explorer**
- Game loop basics
- Keyboard input
- Collision detection

**Quest 2: Bouncing Ball**
- Physics simulation
- Velocity and gravity
- Multiple objects

**Estimated time:** 8 hours
:::

::: {.quest-card}
### 🎯 Intermediate Games (Quests 3-5)
Build classic games

**Quest 3: Pong**
- Two-player mechanics
- AI opponents
- Scoring systems

**Quest 4: Snake**
- Grid-based movement
- Data structures (deques)
- Self-collision

**Quest 5: Flappy Bird**
- Infinite scrolling
- Jump mechanics
- Procedural generation

**Estimated time:** 12 hours
:::

::: {.quest-card}
### 🚀 Advanced Games (Quests 6-7)
Master complex systems

**Quest 6: Frogger**
- Multi-lane systems
- Platform riding
- Zone collision

**Quest 7: Dungeon Crawler**
- Tile-based maps
- Camera systems
- AI pathfinding
- Inventory systems

**Estimated time:** 10 hours
:::

</div>

---

## 🚀 Quick Start

Ready to build games? Choose your path:

::: {.highlight-box .highlight-info}
### Option 1: Start Quest 1 → (Recommended!)

Jump right into building your first game. We'll guide you through setup.

[Begin Quest 1: Maze Explorer →](quests/01-maze-explorer.qmd){.btn .btn-primary}
:::

::: {.highlight-box .highlight-success}
### Option 2: Setup First

Want to prepare your environment before starting? Get everything ready.

[Setup Guide →](setup.qmd){.btn .btn-success}
:::

::: {.highlight-box .highlight-warning}
### Option 3: Browse All Games

See the complete game development curriculum.

[View All Quests →](all-quests.qmd){.btn .btn-warning}
:::

---

## 💻 What You Need

::: {.feature-grid}

::: {.feature-card}
### 🐍 Python 3.12+
Recent Python version for best Pygame compatibility
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### 🎮 Pygame 2.5.2
The game development framework we'll use
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### ⚡ UV Package Manager
Fast, modern Python dependency management
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### 💻 Code Editor
VS Code, PyCharm, or any Python-friendly editor
:::

:::

---

## 🎓 Who Is This Track For?

::: {.audience-grid}

::: {.audience-card}
### 🐍 Python Developers
Know Python? Ready to build games? Perfect!
:::

::: {.audience-card}
### 🎮 Aspiring Game Devs
Want to break into game development? Start here.
:::

::: {.audience-card}
### 👨‍🎓 Students
Learn programming concepts through fun projects.
:::

::: {.audience-card}
### 🧑‍🏫 Educators
Engage students with interactive game projects.
:::

:::

---

## 🎮 Why Pygame?

::: {.advantage-grid}

**🐍 Python-Based**  
Use familiar Python syntax — no new language to learn

**📚 Great for Learning**  
Perfect balance of power and simplicity

**🎨 2D Focus**  
Master fundamentals before 3D complexity

**🆓 Free & Open**  
Active community, extensive documentation

**🚀 Rapid Prototyping**  
Create games quickly — see results fast

**📦 Cross-Platform**  
Your games run on Windows, Mac, Linux

:::

---

## 🗺️ Learning Path

Your game development journey:

```{mermaid}
graph TB
    A[Python Basics] --> B[Quest 1: Maze Explorer]
    B --> C[Quest 2: Bouncing Ball]
    C --> D[Quest 3: Pong]
    D --> E[Quest 4: Snake]
    E --> F[Quest 5: Flappy Bird]
    F --> G[Quest 6: Frogger]
    G --> H[Quest 7: Dungeon Crawler]
    H --> I[Your Own Games!]
    
    style A fill:#10b981
    style B fill:#3b82f6
    style C fill:#8b5cf6
    style D fill:#ec4899
    style E fill:#f59e0b
    style F fill:#ef4444
    style G fill:#06b6d4
    style H fill:#a855f7
    style I fill:#10b981
```

---

## 📚 Track Resources

::: {.resource-links}
- [📖 All Game Quests](all-quests.qmd)
- [🔧 Setup & Installation](setup.qmd)
- [📥 Download Projects](downloads.qmd)
- [🎮 Game Assets](assets.qmd)
- [❓ Pygame FAQ](faq.qmd)
- [📘 Pygame Documentation](https://www.pygame.org/docs/)
:::

---

## 🎯 Learning Tips

::: {.tip-box}
### 💡 Master Game Development

1. **Type the code** - Don't copy/paste, type it yourself
2. **Run frequently** - Test after each change
3. **Experiment** - Modify values, add features
4. **Complete games** - Finish each before moving on
5. **Add your ideas** - Make games your own
6. **Debug patiently** - Game bugs are great teachers
7. **Play your games** - Test thoroughly, iterate
:::

---

## 🏆 What Makes Games Tick?

Every game (even triple-A titles) follows these core patterns you'll learn:

::: {.game-patterns}

### The Game Loop 🔁
```
Input → Update → Render → Repeat
```

### Collision Detection 💥
```
Check if objects overlap → Respond
```

### State Management 🎮
```
Menu → Playing → Paused → Game Over
```

### Event Handling ⌨️
```
Listen → Detect → React
```

:::

---

## 🗺️ After This Track

Once you complete the Pygame track:

::: {.next-steps}

### 🎨 Add Art & Sound
Learn to create or integrate:
- Custom sprites and animations
- Sound effects and music
- Particle systems
- Visual effects

---

### 🌐 Publish Your Games
Share with the world:
- Upload to itch.io
- Create web versions with Pygbag
- Package for distribution
- Join game jams

---

### 🚀 Advanced Game Dev
Level up to:
- 3D games with Pygame-gl or Panda3D
- Godot Engine (excellent 2D/3D)
- Unity or Unreal (industry standard)
- Game design patterns

:::

---

## 🤝 Community & Support

::: {.callout-tip}
## Need Help?

- 📧 [Email Support](mailto:obonhamcarter@allegheny.edu)
- 📖 [FAQ](faq.qmd) - Common Pygame questions
- 💬 [Pygame Community](https://www.pygame.org/wiki/about)
- 🐛 [Report Issues](https://github.com/obonhamcarter/CS_Quest_Adventures/issues)
:::

---

## ⚠️ Important Notes

::: {.callout-warning}
### About Pygame Games

**Browser Limitations:** Unlike Python code, Pygame games cannot run directly in your browser. You must:
1. Install Python and Pygame locally
2. Download and run the game files
3. Or use experimental tools like Pygbag (for web export)

**The good news:** Setup is straightforward, and you'll have complete game projects you can run, modify, and share!
:::

---

## ➡️ Ready to Build Games?

Your game development quest awaits! Click below to start:

::: {.cta-large}
[🚀 Start Quest 1: The Maze of Ancient Wisdom](quests/01-maze-explorer.qmd){.btn .btn-primary .btn-lg}
:::

Or [view all game quests →](all-quests.qmd) to see what you'll build.

---

::: {.footer-note}
**About This Track**

Created by [Oliver Bonham-Carter](https://www.oliverbonhamcarter.com)  
Department of Computer and Information Science, Allegheny College  

Questions? [obonhamcarter@allegheny.edu](mailto:obonhamcarter@allegheny.edu)
:::
